source: Curated (game modes are gameplay rules, not exported in the Mod Helper dump)

modes.description: Default progression with all towers available. Round ranges by difficulty: Easy 1-40, Medium 1-60, Hard 1-80. Starting lives 200 (Easy) / 150 (Medium) / 100 (Hard).
Easy-difficulty mode: only Primary-class towers may be placed.
Easy-difficulty mode starting at round 31 with a fixed lump of cash and no income — win with what you can afford up front.
restrictions: No income — popping bloons earns no cash

modes.description: Medium-difficulty mode: only Military-class towers may be placed.
Medium-difficulty mode: bloons stream in continuously with no round breaks and rounds cannot be started manually.
restrictions: Continuous bloon stream — no round breaks

modes.description: Medium-difficulty mode: bloons enter from the normal exit and travel the track in reverse.
restrictions: Bloons travel the track in reverse (enter from the exit)

modes.description: Hard-difficulty mode: only Magic-class towers may be placed.
Hard-difficulty mode: all MOAB-class bloons (MOAB, BFB, ZOMG, DDT, BAD) have double health.
restrictions: All MOAB-class bloons have double health

modes.description: Hard-difficulty mode: all cash earned (from pops and income) is halved.
Hard-difficulty mode: rounds use an alternate, generally tougher bloon composition than the default round set.
Hard-difficulty endgame mode: rounds 6-100, 1 life, full bloon speed, higher tower prices, no continues and no Monkey Knowledge.
Hard-mode endgame challenge. The acronym: no Continues, Hearts lost (1 life), Income, Monkey knowledge, Powers, or Selling.
restrictions: No income (no Banana Farm / Monkey City cash generation)

modes.description: Practice mode with unlimited cash and lives for testing tower placements and interactions.
restrictions: Unlimited cash and lives (practice only — no rewards)
